rulings.Case Title: DAN LIU ET AL VS. MEI HUI HUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Of Motion For Leave Of Court To File Amendment To Complaint Naming Does 1 And 2
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF DAN LIU, JUN REN, YANG YANG Of Motion For Leave Of Court To File Amendment To Complaint Naming Does 1 And 2 is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013 based on the proof of service by mail. Notice of Settlement on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS ET AL VS. EMPORIUM MALL LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Instructions To Receiver; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT EMPORIUM MALL LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILTY, S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A DELAWARE Notice Of Motion For Instructions To Receiver; Points And Authorities is GRANTED. Receiver shall name the Defendants' as the Additional Insured's within 5 business days of this ruling. Receiver shall provide all copies of the Insurance Certificates received by the receiver since April 1, 2024 within 5 business days of this ruling. Copies of any Insurance Certificates received in the future shall be provided to all parties and filed with the Court within 5 business day of receipt by the Receiver. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS ET AL VS. EMPORIUM MALL LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Instructions To Receiver; Points And Authorities (Additional Named Insureds)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT EMPORIUM MALL LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILTY, S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A DELAWARE Motion For Instructions To Receiver; Points And Authorities (Additional Named Insureds) is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Receiver shall reimburse the Defendants for insurance premiums through March 31, 2024 within 30 days of Defendants providing documentation that receiver needs regarding the premiums. No later than 9:00 a.m. on May 23, 2024 receiver to file a declaration identifying necessary documents that have not already been provided. (It appears that some documentation has been provided as the discussion of the cost of insurance has been previously before the Court). Motion is otherwise DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. EPIC PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing A Rents. Issues And Profits Receiver For The Real Property Commonly Known As 25 And 33 Drumm Street, San Francisco, California, And A Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of The Receiver; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities is continued to June 28, 2024. No later than June 7, 2024 the moving party to file a list specifying the document to be produced by the Defendant generally referenced in the reply as "all required books, records, income and expense statements, and financials for the Property." No later than June 20, 2024 Defendant to produced such documents to the Plaintiff and file a declaration with the Court no later than 5:00 p.m. on June 20, 2024 attesting to such production. No later than June 26, 2024 Plaintiff to file a reply either confirming the receipt of all requested documents or specifying the documents that have not been produced. Additionally, no later than June 20, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) and declaration(s) with Plaintiff's/proposed receiver's suggestions as to the hiring of a different listing broker who is ready and willing to market the property for rent. Such broker(s) shall be identified and the estimated time for locating new tenants shall be provided. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DEVENDRA PATEL VS. BRAD ALLEN VANDYKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Brad Allen Vandyke To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF DEVENDRA PATEL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Brad Allen Vandyke To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents is GRANTED, subject to opposition.
